title: Fold-safe latent tyre-state filter from a state-space degradation model
summary: Recent F1 work models lap time as observed output from a latent tyre/degradation state with pit stops as structural resets.
rationale: Recent F1 work models lap time as observed output from a latent tyre/degradation state with pit stops as structural resets. That is a better match to this task than raw cumulative degradation because the target is effectively about hidden stint state, not just current telemetry snapshot.
implementation_hint: Inside each training fold only, fit a simple per-compound/per-race causal filter on each driver-stint using past laps only: estimate latent pace loss, one-step degradation slope, shock/outlier flag, and post-reset recovery. Export those filtered states as new numeric features to XGB/CatBoost; never fit the filter on validation races before scoring them.
expected_effect: Should improve separation of true end-of-stint risk from noisy slow laps and may especially help on races where raw degradation is noisy or negative-valued.
risk: Easy to leak if the smoother uses future laps or is fit on full data; also possible that a lightweight filter adds little beyond current handcrafted tyre-life proxies.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
